calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-17-2018 Vacated

Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Apr-27-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Defendant Long's motion for judgment on the pleadings on plaintiff Cavalry SPC I, LLC's complaint is DENIED. The face of the complaint does not disclose that the action is time barred. Paragraphs 9 and 17 of the complaint allege that Long defaulted on the account on 9/1/13. Under CCP 337, Cavalry had four years to commence this action from that date and timely filed this action on 5/30/17. Long fails to explain how the alleged burglary altered the running of the statute of limitations. Cavalry is not required to plead the particular details of its purchase of the Citibank account. In addition, Exhibit 1 attached to the complaint indicates that the sale occurred in 2/17. The court finds that Long's citation to Komarova v. National Credit Acceptance, Inc. (2009) 175 Cal.App.4th 324 is inapplicable.

The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, e-mail: torreanom@aol.org (L&M302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration; Memo Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Orzell Long In Support
Off Calendar Apr-06-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances are made by or for the parties in the case.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

RULING: DEFENDANT ORZELL LONG's motion for reconsideration is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. Defendant declares under penalty of perjury that he did not receive plaintiff's opposition to defendant's motion for judgment on the pleadings and therefore did not have a chance to file a reply brief. The court vacates the underlying order denying the motion for judgment on the pleadings that was entered on February 21, 2018. The court reschedules the motion for judgment on the pleadings for hearing on April 27, 2018 before Judge Ulmer at 9:30 a.m. in department 302. The court infers that defendant now has a copy of the opposition to the motion for judgment on the pleadings as his declaration in support of the motion for reconsideration refers to the text of that document. Defendant has leave to file his reply brief in support of the motion for judgment on the pleadings by April 19, 2018. All parties shall provide courtesy copies of their filings regarding the motion for judgment on the pleadings pursuant to SF Local Rule 2.7B with a cover letter that reflects the April 27, 2018 hearing date.

Defendant is to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court which complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Peremptory Challenge; Memo Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Apr-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances are made by or for the parties in the case.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

RULING: As to DEFENDANT ORZELL LONG's Motion For Peremptory Challenge, Judge Kahn strikes the challenge for cause pursuant to CCP 170.1 as being legally deficient on its face as it fails to disclose a legal ground for disqualification. (See CCP 170.4(b); Urias v. Harris Farms, Inc. (1991) 234 Cal.App.3d 415, 420.) Judge Kahn accepts the CCP 170.6 challenge.

Defendant is to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court which complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/HK)

Defendant Orzell Long'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Memo Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Feb-21-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Viviane Leung Lerche, Esq. 415-680-5731 specially appearing for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested.

DEFENDANT ORZELL LONG's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

Defendant Orzell Long's motion for judgment on the pleadings on plaintiff Cavalry SPC I, LLC's complaint is denied. The face of the complaint does not disclose that either of the claims alleged in the complaint is time-barred. Paragraphs 9 and 17 of the complaint allege that Mr. Long defaulted on the account on September 1, 2013. Per CCP 337, Cavalry had four years to commence this action from that date and timely filed this action on May 30, 2017. Mr. Long fails to explain how the alleged burglary altered the running of the statute of limitations. Cavalry is not required to plead the particular details of its purchase of the Citibank account. Exhibit 1 to the complaint shows the sale occurred in February 2017. Komarova v. National Credit Acceptance, Inc. (2009) 175 Cal.App.4th 324 is inapplicable.
Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for Cavalry is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D302/HK)
